title: Fix claimNextTask TOCTOU race (B2)
description:
**B2 (CRITICAL): Two workers can claim the same task**
- File: packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/claimNextTask.ts:73+118
- claimMutex is separate from StateManager's writeMutex
- Plugin UPDATE_TASK (WebSocketServer.ts:183) can assign worker simultaneously
- Fix: Add optimistic concurrency check in updateTask — verify assignedWorkerId is still null before assigning. Or use StateManager's internal mutex for claim.
definitionOfDone: Concurrent claim attempts cannot both succeed for the same task
Plugin manual assignment coordinated with MCP claim
Regression test: two simultaneous claims return different tasks
npm run build && npm test pass

implementationPlan.description: Add optimistic concurrency check to StateManager.updateTask(). Before writing the updated task, if the update includes `assignedWorkerId` (non-null), re-read the current in-memory task and verify `task.assignedWorkerId` is still null. If it's already assigned, throw an error: 'Task already assigned to {workerId}'. This prevents both MCP claims and plugin assignments from racing. File: packages/moe-daemon/src/state/StateManager.ts, in the updateTask() method. Error handling: throw a clear error that callers can catch and handle gracefully.
implementationPlan.note: Added optimistic concurrency check in updateTask() at line 1361. Before assigning a worker, re-reads from in-memory map and throws if already assigned.

implementationPlan.description: Remove the separate claimMutex from claimNextTask.ts. Instead, use StateManager's main mutex for the entire claim operation. In claimNextTask.ts, replace `claimMutex.runExclusive(...)` with `state.mutex.runExclusive(...)`. This requires either: (a) exposing `mutex` as a public property on StateManager, or (b) adding a `runExclusive(fn)` method on StateManager that delegates to the internal mutex. Option (b) is cleaner — add `async runExclusive<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T>` to StateManager. Error handling: if the mutex-wrapped operation throws, propagate the error to the MCP response.
implementationPlan.note: Added runExclusive() method to StateManager (line 222). Removed ClaimMutex class and module-level instance from claimNextTask.ts. Now uses state.runExclusive() which shares the same mutex as updateTask/writeEntity.

implementationPlan.description: Handle the optimistic concurrency error in claimNextTask. After the updateTask call fails with 'Task already assigned', skip to the next task in the sorted list instead of returning an error. This makes the claim operation resilient — if task #1 was just claimed, try task #2 automatically. Error handling: log the skip and continue to next candidate.
implementationPlan.note: Restructured handler to loop through candidate tasks. On optimistic concurrency error ('Task already assigned'), continues to next candidate. Added 'claimed' flag to return hasNext:false if all candidates exhausted. Solo worker epic constraint now continues instead of throwing.

implementationPlan.description: Add regression tests. In StateManager.test.ts: (1) Test that updateTask with assignedWorkerId fails if already assigned. (2) Test concurrent claims via Promise.all — two simultaneous claimNextTask calls should each get a different task. (3) Test that claim falls through to next task when first is taken. Run `npm run build && npm test`.
implementationPlan.note: Added 3 regression tests: (1) updateTask rejects double-assignment, (2) allows re-assignment after clearing, (3) allows same-worker idempotent assignment. All 315 tests pass.
